questions.question: What is the main goal of distress tolerance skills?

options.text: To get through a crisis moment safely without making things worse
To figure out why you are feeling overwhelmed
To distract yourself until the feeling goes away

questions.explanation: Distress tolerance is not about fixing or eliminating the emotion. It is about surviving the crisis moment safely so you can deal with the underlying situation when you are calmer and thinking more clearly.

questions.question: How does the Temperature component of TIPP help during a crisis?

options.text: It warms your muscles to release tension
It activates the mammalian dive response, which slows the heart rate
It raises your blood pressure to sharpen focus
It numbs physical pain so you can concentrate

questions.explanation: Cold temperature activates the dive response, a hard-wired mammalian reflex that slows the heart rate when the face is submerged in cold water. This rapidly changes your body chemistry and helps bring you out of the emotional red zone.

questions.question: What does the Paced Breathing component of TIPP do to your nervous system?

options.text: It activates your sympathetic nervous system to increase alertness
It activates your parasympathetic nervous system by slowing the exhale
It blocks adrenaline from reaching the brain
It increases oxygen to keep you awake and focused

questions.explanation: Slowing your exhale directly activates the parasympathetic nervous system — your body's brake pedal. This signals to your brain that danger has passed and it is safe to calm down.

questions.question: What does Paired Muscle Relaxation involve?

options.text: Stretching each muscle group slowly for 60 seconds
Massaging tense muscles with a warm compress
Tensing a muscle group while breathing in, then releasing it completely while breathing out
Shaking your limbs to release stored tension

questions.explanation: Paired Muscle Relaxation works by first intensely tensing a muscle group while breathing in, then releasing the tension suddenly while breathing out. This tension-and-release pairing forces the muscles into a more relaxed state than they were in before.

questions.question: Think about a recent moment when your emotions felt overwhelming. Which component of the TIPP skill — Temperature, Intense Exercise, Paced Breathing, or Paired Muscle Relaxation — do you think would have been most helpful in that moment, and why?
questions.explanation: There is no single right answer here. The best TIPP component for you depends on what was driving the intensity — racing heart, adrenaline surge, or physical tension. Reflecting on which one fits your experience helps you reach for it more easily when the next moment comes.

title: Calming the Storm: Crisis Mode and the TIPP Skill - Quiz
